mission.title: Install and Configure Headlamp on Kubernetes
mission.description: Headlamp is an easy-to-use and extensible Kubernetes web UI that provides a user-friendly interface for managing Kubernetes resources. Installing Headlamp allows you to visualize and interact with your Kubernetes clusters more effectively.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Headlamp Helm chart repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add headlamp https://charts.headlamp.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Headlamp using Helm
steps.description:
Install Headlamp in your Kubernetes cluster with the following command, creating a new namespace called 'headlamp':
```bash
helm install headlamp headlamp/headlamp --namespace headlamp --create-namespace --version 0.40.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Headlamp pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace headlamp
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access Headlamp
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Headlamp UI from your local machine:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/headlamp --namespace headlamp 8080:80
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Headlamp deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment configuration:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment headlamp --namespace headlamp
```
Then, add the following under the `spec.template.spec.containers.resources` section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Headlamp release from your cluster:
```bash
helm uninstall headlamp --namespace headlamp
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) related to Headlamp:
```bash
kubectl delete crd headlamp.k8s.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'headlamp' namespace to clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace headlamp
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration:
```bash
helm get values headlamp --namespace headlamp > headlamp-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Headlamp chart:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Headlamp
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Headlamp to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade headlamp headlamp/headlamp --namespace headlamp --version 0.40.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Headlamp pods are running successfully after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace headlamp
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the Headlamp pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace headlamp
```
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources available or adjust the resource requests in the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Headlamp UI not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Headlamp UI, ensure that the port-forward command is running. If it is, check the service:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace headlamp
```
Make sure the service is correctly configured and running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: ImagePullBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see an ImagePullBackOff error, check the image name and tag:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace headlamp
```
Ensure that the image exists in the specified registry and that your cluster can access it.

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff on Headlamp pods
troubleshooting.description:
If the Headlamp pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace headlamp
```
Look for any error messages that indicate what might be wrong with the configuration.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Headlamp will result in a running instance of the Headlamp UI, accessible via port-forwarding on your local machine. You should see the Headlamp dashboard when navigating to http://localhost:8080.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add headlamp https://charts.headlamp.dev
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm package manager installed.
